english: 1. a long-measure, a fathom, = 6 feet, ₒdom-gáṅ ('dom gang) one fathom, S.g., as the usual length of a man, = k̇ru bżi (khru bzhi); śiṅ ₒdom dó (shing 'dom do) a piece of wood two fathoms long Dzl.; ₒdom bču̇i doṅ ('dom bcu'i dong) a well ten fathoms deep; ₒdóm-gyis ('doms gyis), or ₒdóms-su jál-ba ('doms su 'jal ba) to measure by fathoms Cs.; ₒdom-gaṅ-gru-bżi ('dom gang gru bzhi) 1. adj. measuring a square fathom; also a cubic fathom; 2. sbst. a strong jail or dungeon. — 2. imp. of ₒdam-pa ('dam pa) to choose.

the pudenda, privities, regio pubis, ₒdoms(-kyi)-spu ('doms kyi spu) the hair of that region, ₒdoms-spu ṭóg-pa ('doms spu thog pa) to pluck out such hair Cs.; rṅa-ma ₒdoms óg-tu j̇úg-pa (rnga ma 'doms 'og tu 'jug pa) col. to take to one's heels; ₒdoms-stóṅ ('doms stong) vulg. without breeches; sdoms-lpágs (sdoms lpags) foreskin, prepuce (?); ₒdoms - ɣtsáṅ (-ma) ('doms gtsang ma) C. a pure virgin; a nun; ₒdoms - ɣtsáṅ - pa ('doms gtsang pa) a chaste monk (if not rather sdom (sdom) is meant); ₒdoms-rás ('doms ras) (also čar - rás (char ras) Cs.) a small apron to cover the privy parts Cs.

pf. and imp. dor (dor) (cog. to ɣtór - ba (gtor ba), stór - ba (stor ba), byi - dór (byi dor), pýag - dár (phyag dar)). 1. to throw or cast away, like ɣtór - ba (gtor ba) and ₒbór-ba ('bor ba) Stg.; esp. to throw out, to eject, spittle, frq.; dri-ču ₒdór-ba (dri chu 'dor ba) to make water Glr.; fig. srog ₒdór-ba (srog 'dor ba) to fling away one's life Dzl.; to sweep out or away Dzl., Stg. — 2. (opp. to lén - pa (len pa), bżéd - pa (bzhed pa)) to decline, refuse, reject, despise, things offered Dzl.; to reject, a reading, a passage Gram.; to disapprove, of an action as immoral; blaṅ-dór (blang dor), ₒdor-léṅ ('dor len), accepting and rejecting, deciding for or against, e.g. dge-sdig-gi (dge sdig gi) Glr. — 3. to subtract, dór-bai lḥág-ma (dor ba'i lhag ma) Wdk. the remainder left after subtracting; perh. also to divide. — 4. srog ₒdór-ba (srog 'dor ba) also signifies: to endanger life, or to deprive of life, used e.g. of diseases S.g.; góm-pa ₒdór-ba (gom pa 'dor ba) (= ₒbór-ba ('bor ba)), to pace, to step, to stride, frq.; dmód-pa ₒdor-ba (dmod pa 'dor ba) v. dmód-pa (dmod pa).
Lex., fertile ground or soil Sch.

1. adj., C.: *ḍá-te* (da te), similar, equal (which two notions gen. are not strictly distinguished from each other); ₒdrá-ba ₒdi-dag ('dra ba 'di dag) these equal things, for: these comparisons, Pth.; k̇yed ɣnyis ₒdrá-bar ₒdug (khyed gnyis 'dra bar 'dug), ₒdra - ba yin ('dra ba yin), ₒdrao ('dra'o), you two resemble each other very much; with a pleon. mnyam (mnyam): riṅ-túṅ mnyám-la ₒdrá-ba (ring thung mnyam la 'dra ba) equally long Dzl.; gen. with daṅ (dang) or accus., seldom with termin., in various applications: k̇yed(daṅ) ₒdrá-ba ni (khyed dang 'dra ba ni) your equals Dzl.; bud-méd-du ₒdrá-bai náṅ-na (bud med du 'dra ba'i nang na) amongst woman-like, effeminate (men), Dzl.; ₒdi̇i byin tsáṅs-pa daṅ ₒdrao ('di'i byin tshangs pa dang 'dra'o) his brightness is equal to (that of) Brahma Dzl.; ɣżán-gyi dón-laaṅ ráṅ-gi ₒdrar séms-pa (gzhan gyi don la'ang rang gi 'drar sems pa) esteeming our neighbour's advantage as high as our own S.g.; ṭams-čád-la bu ɣčig-pa daṅ drao (thams cad la bu gcig pa dang dra'o) he behaved to all as (to) an only son Dzl.; with a negative: ɣżan yaṅ de daṅ ₒdrá-ste ɣnáṅ-ba med (gzhan yang de dang 'dra ste gnang ba med) others shall allow it just as little as he himself Dzl.; Saṅs-rgyás daṅ ₒdrá-bar byá-bai ṗyir (sangs rgyas dang 'dra bar bya ba'i phyir) in order to be equal to Buddha, to come up with Buddha Dzl.; brtsigs-pa mi ₒdra skyés-pa ₒdra (brtsigs pa mi 'dra skyes pa 'dra) not as if (it had been) built, but as if it had grown up spontaneously Glr.; bdag ₒdra bud-méd blo-dmán kyaṅ (bdag 'dra bud med blo dman kyang) even a stupid woman like myself; skra ₒdrá-ba yód-dam (skra 'dra ba yod dam) whether any thing like hair is still left? Mil.; téṅ-ro ₒdra rnyed (teng ro 'dra rnyed) he found the remnants of a carcass or something like it Mil.; ro daṅ ₒdrá-ba (ro dang 'dra ba) as much as dead Wdn.; mnyán-pa daṅ ₒdrá-bai bśes-ɣnyéṅ (mnyan pa dang 'dra ba'i bshes gnyen) a teacher like as a ferryman (conveying to the shores of happiness) Thgy.; rtag - rtág ₒdra yaṅ (rtag rtag 'dra yang) seemingly eternal Mil; skyid-skyíd ₒdra yaṅ (skyid skyid 'dra yang) even if it appears a blessing Mil.; rúṅ-ba daṅ ₒdrá - na (rung ba dang 'dra na) if it appears feasible Dzl.; ster dgós-pa ₒdra (ster dgos pa 'dra) it seems I shall be obliged to give it Glr.; da-lán k̇yod nús-pa če-čé ₒdra bżin byúṅ-ste (da lan khyod nus pa che che 'dra bzhin byung ste) as your strength this time at least seems to be rather great Mil.; k̇yed slu-slú ₒdra (khyed slu slu 'dra) you might easily be ensnared Mil.; mi-ₒdrá-ba (mi 'dra ba) unequal, unlike, different, sṅon-čád daṅ mi ₒdrá-bar (sngon chad dang mi 'dra bar) quite otherwise than formerly Dzl.; čós-pa mi ₒdrá-bar (chos pa mi 'dra bar) not like, not befitting, a priest Mil.; various, several, *k̇a-zé mi-ḍá-wa* (kha ze mi da wa) C. several dishes; ₒdí-ₒdra-ba ('di 'dra ba), dé-ₒdra-ba (de 'dra ba) such; dé-dras (de dras), (*dhéṅ-de* (dheng de) C. vulg.) so, thus; čì-ₒdra-ba (ci 'dra ba), j̇í-ₒdra-ba (ji 'dra ba) of what kind (qualis), čì-ₒdra čig légs- (ci 'dra cig legs)
